key_insight: WEST is an open-source, GPU-scalable excited-state simulation code that unifies GW, Bethe-Salpeter, TDDFT, and related methods without explicit virtual states.
engineering_relevance: It enables accurate large-scale predictions of quasiparticle, optical, and defect-related excited-state properties for materials systems with thousands of atoms.
method: Common algorithmic framework for GW, QDE, BSE, and TDDFT
Uses density functional/density matrix perturbation theory plus low-rank screening and exact-exchange representations
Hierarchical parallelization and GPU acceleration for near-ideal strong scaling
evidence_signal: The abstract highlights representative applications and near-ideal strong scaling to thousands of GPUs, with systems exceeding 1,000 atoms.
watch_item: Performance and accuracy depend on sophisticated implementations of low-rank/localization approximations and GPU-aware workflows, which may limit ease of adoption or portability.
paper_title: The WEST code for large-scale excited-state materials simulations
